# Ralph Progress Log — Marketify
Started: (will be replaced on first run)
---

## Codebase Patterns
*Append-only consolidated learnings. Each future iteration reads this BEFORE doing anything.*

- **Authoritative specs are `docs/product-plan.md`, `docs/design.md`, `docs/tech-architecture.md`.** When the story is ambiguous, read the relevant section there. Do not invent product behavior.
- **Use Supabase MCP, not the supabase CLI**, for migrations, queries, edge functions, and type generation. Migrations are append-only and named `us_NNN_description`.
- **Use mobile-mcp for any UI verification.** Never claim a UI story is done without a screenshot from `mobile_take_screenshot`.
- **Use the `feature-dev:code-reviewer` subagent before every commit.** It catches drift across iterations that you can't see in your fresh context.
- **Delegate exploration.** If a story would have you read >3 files, spawn the `Explore` subagent first and work from its summary.
- **Always regenerate `src/types/supabase.ts`** after any schema migration. Commit the regenerated file in the same commit as the migration.
- **Use `bun`, not npm/yarn/pnpm.** It is what's installed locally.
- **JWT secret is `MARKETIFY_JWT_SECRET`**, separate from Supabase's built-in JWT secret. We sign our own per docs/product-plan.md §2.
- **Apify key is `APIFY_KEY`** in `.env`. Read via `process.env.APIFY_KEY` or `Deno.env.get("APIFY_KEY")`. Never paste into source.
- **Edge functions live in `supabase/functions/<name>/index.ts`** with shared utils under `supabase/functions/_shared/`.
- **Component organization:** primitives in `src/components/primitives/`, screen-specific in `src/screens/<screen>/`, shared composites in `src/components/shared/`.
- **No emoji in product UI** — Lucide icons only (per docs/design.md).
- **One role per account in v1** — no dual-role logic.
- **Run `get_advisors({type:'security'})` after every RLS or schema change.** Address every error-level finding before commit.
- **`expo lint` default glob is `['src', 'app', 'components']`.** Empty subdirs with only `.gitkeep` cause eslint to exit 2 ("all files ignored"). Keep at least one lintable `.ts`/`.tsx` file in any of those roots (`src/index.ts` with `export {};` was used for US-001).
- **Expo bootstrap workaround:** `create-expo-app .` refuses to run when the target dir has dotfiles/dirs it considers hostile. Bootstrap into a sibling folder (e.g. `.expo-init`), then move the generated files up. Preserves `.claude/`, `.env`, `docs/`, `scripts/`.
- **Reanimated 4.x babel plugin is `react-native-worklets/plugin`** (not the legacy `react-native-reanimated/plugin`). It lives in the `react-native-worklets` package — pin it explicitly via `bunx expo install react-native-worklets` so `bun install --production` won't drop it. Included by default in fresh Expo SDK 54 templates.
- **Prefer `bunx expo install <pkg>`** over `bun add` for native modules — it pins the SDK-compatible version. Expo's install runs `bun add` under the hood when a `bun.lock` is present.
- **App entry is `"main": "expo-router/entry"`** in package.json. The old `index.ts` + `App.tsx` from US-001 were removed. Root layout lives at `app/_layout.tsx`; initial route at `app/index.tsx`.
- **Expo Go + SDK 54 + new-arch + `<Stack>` from expo-router throws at runtime**: `Exception in HostFunction: TypeError: expected dynamic type 'boolean', but had type 'string'`. For US-002 the root uses `<Slot />` as the minimal root. US-031 will revisit with a dev-client build (`expo run:ios`) where Stack + Tabs should work.
- **iOS simulator boot flow for mobile-mcp verification:** `xcrun simctl boot <name>` → `open -a Simulator` → `bunx expo start --ios` in background → `xcrun simctl openurl <udid> exp://127.0.0.1:8081` if Expo Go doesn't auto-open the project. mobile-mcp's `mobile_open_url` blocks `exp://` schemes by default, so shell out.
- **Supabase project:** `marketify` (ref=`[REDACTED_SUPABASE_PROJECT_REF]`, region=`ap-southeast-1`). All MCP DB calls use this `project_id`. Pre-installed extensions: pgcrypto, uuid-ossp, pg_stat_statements, supabase_vault, pg_graphql. pg_cron + citext available on request.
- **Install extensions `with schema extensions`** (e.g. `create extension if not exists citext with schema extensions`) to keep advisors clean. `pg_cron` is the exception: Supabase requires it in `pg_catalog`, leave it unqualified.
- **Enum canonical list is docs/tech-architecture.md §4.2** (lines 399-427). After generating types, verify `src/types/supabase.ts` Constants block matches it exactly.
- **Local migration files live under `supabase/migrations/` with `YYYYMMDDHHMMSS_us_NNN_description.sql`.** Keep filename and MCP migration `name` (no timestamp prefix) consistent. Commit every applied migration — `list_migrations` is the DB side, the file is the source of truth for fresh applies.
- **`app_private` schema was created in US-003** via a companion migration (`us_003_app_private_schema`). Revoke is already in place for `anon` and `authenticated`. Triggers / security-definer functions in later stories should target this schema.
- **Reference `citext` as `extensions.citext` in every migration.** US-003 installed it with `with schema extensions`; the default `search_path` used by migrations does not include that schema, so an unqualified `citext` fails to resolve.
- **Supabase auto-enables RLS on new `public.*` tables** on this project. New table migrations will show up in `get_advisors({type:'security'})` as INFO-level `rls_enabled_no_policy` (not `rls_disabled`). These INFO findings are benign until policies land in US-009 — no commit-blocking action needed.
- **`listing_conditions` is a single data-driven table**, not separate pre/post tables. Discriminated by `condition_kind` enum ('pre' | 'post') per docs/tech-architecture.md §4.7 block 8. Pre-condition rows use metrics like `min_followers`; post-condition rows use `post_*` metrics. The story US-005 text named separate tables but the spec wins.
- **Circular FK pattern:** `listings.current_version_id` references `listing_versions(id)` but both tables are in the same migration. Create the `listings` table with the column nullable, create `listing_versions`, then `alter table public.listings add constraint ... foreign key ...`. Spec follows this pattern (§4.7 lines 659-661).
- **Supabase defaults views to `security_invoker=off` (effectively DEFINER)**; it will fail the `security_definer_view` advisor. Every view we create must be followed with `alter view <name> set (security_invoker = on)` to match the project standard at §4.1 line 375. Applied to `submission_reuse_view` in US-006.
- **US-009 TODO — `submission_reuse_view` correctness under RLS:** with `security_invoker=on`, the self-join on `public.submissions other` will be filtered by the caller's `submissions_lister_read` policy, so a lister's `reuse_count` will only count reuses on submissions *they themselves can see* — not cross-lister reuses, which is the ReuseBadge's entire purpose (spec §4.6). The spec comment at tech-architecture.md:760 said "Security-def view so RLS on base tables doesn't mask the count" but the body has no lister filter baked in — a spec contradiction. Resolution options: (a) replace the view with an `app_private` SECURITY DEFINER set-returning function that takes a submission_id and returns the count with base-table RLS bypassed; (b) keep the view but scope its input with a parameter. Must be addressed alongside the RLS migration in US-009 before the ReuseBadge UI (US-058, US-065) ships.
- **Trigger function hardening pattern (mandatory for every new trigger / PL/pgSQL function in this repo):**
  1. Declare `set search_path = ''` on the function (CREATE OR REPLACE drops prior SET clauses, so restate it on every redefinition).
  2. With empty `search_path`, qualify EVERY user-defined ref: tables `public.<tbl>`, types/enum casts `public.<type>` (e.g. `'tiktok'::public.platform`), `app_private.<fn>` for sibling functions.
  3. For pg_catalog **functions** (`now()`, `hashtextextended()`, `pg_advisory_xact_lock()`, etc.): bare is fine — `pg_catalog` is always implicitly searched even with empty `search_path`. Schema-qualifying as `pg_catalog.<fn>()` also works for true functions.
  4. **GREATEST / LEAST / COALESCE / NULLIF are SQL parser constructs, NOT functions.** They CANNOT be schema-qualified (`pg_catalog.greatest()` raises 42883). Always write them bare.
  5. SECURITY DEFINER functions especially require empty `search_path` — without it, a caller-controlled search_path can shadow `public.<table>` and divert writes. The advisor `function_search_path_mutable` flags this at WARN.
- **US-008 TODO — out-of-order Apify webhook delivery:** `app_private.denorm_metrics` uses `greatest(metrics_fetched_at, new.fetched_at)` to guard the timestamp column, but the surrounding column UPDATEs (e.g. `tiktok_follower_count`) are unconditional. If a webhook with an older `fetched_at` lands after a newer one (different `apify_run_id`, so the dedup index doesn't catch it), the trigger will overwrite fresh denorm columns with stale values even though the timestamp itself refuses to roll back. The spec §15 has the same gap. Resolution when US-008 (apify-core / webhook receiver) lands: either add `where new.fetched_at >= public.creator_profiles.metrics_fetched_at` to each branch's UPDATE, or guard the whole denorm block on a max(fetched_at) check inside the advisory lock. Update the spec section in the same PR.
- **US-008 nice-to-have — advisory lock collision risk:** `app_private.denorm_metrics` uses single-arg `pg_advisory_xact_lock(hashtextextended(...))`. Two distinct (social_link_id, scrape_mode) strings can collide to the same 64-bit key, causing unrelated creators to serialize unnecessarily. Not a correctness issue (only throughput). Standard fix is the two-arg form `pg_advisory_xact_lock(hashtext(social_link_id::text), hashtext(scrape_mode::text))`. Spec §15 codifies the single-arg form; address as a spec amendment when load matters.
- **`public.events` append-only is a social contract, not a DB-level guarantee.** US-008 reproduces the spec literally (`revoke all on public.events from anon, authenticated`), but `service_role` and `postgres` retain every grant including UPDATE/DELETE — by design per spec §4.4 lines 1083–1085 ("Service role only. No application read/write policy"). Any edge function or trigger that writes to `events` must treat INSERT as the only safe verb; there is no DB trigger that blocks UPDATE/DELETE. If stronger enforcement is ever desired, add a BEFORE UPDATE OR DELETE trigger raising `RAISE EXCEPTION` (this would also block maintenance scripts — considered too restrictive for v1).

---

## 2026-04-17 07:38 — US-001
- Bootstrapped Expo blank-typescript template at repo root; configured app.json (name/slug/scheme + ios.bundleIdentifier / android.package = com.marketify.app); tsconfig strict + `@/*` → `src/*`; added `typecheck` + `lint` scripts.
- Created `src/{components,screens,lib,types}/` each with `.gitkeep`; added `src/index.ts` stub (`export {};`) to satisfy `expo lint`.
- Files changed: `package.json`, `app.json`, `tsconfig.json`, `App.tsx`, `index.ts`, `eslint.config.js`, `.gitignore`, `assets/*`, `bun.lock`, `src/**`
- Quality checks: tsc ✓ / lint ✓ / supabase advisors n/a / mobile-mcp n/a (no UI story yet — US-002 first boots simulator)
- Code-reviewer verdict: approved (no medium+ issues)
- **Learnings for future iterations:**
  - `create-expo-app` refuses to run if target dir has visible files; bootstrap into `.expo-init/` and move files up
  - `expo lint` scans `src|app|components` by default — keep at least one lintable file in any of those dirs or eslint errors "all files ignored"
  - `expo/tsconfig.base` does NOT set `strict`; our own tsconfig adds it
  - The initial `npx expo lint` auto-installs `eslint` + `eslint-config-expo` and auto-writes `eslint.config.js` — noted in package.json devDeps (intentional)
---

## 2026-04-17 08:10 — US-003
- Applied `us_003_baseline_enums` (pgcrypto/citext in `extensions`, pg_cron in `pg_catalog`, all 11 enums per §4.2) and companion `us_003_app_private_schema` (empty schema + revoke) via Supabase MCP on project `marketify` (ref `[REDACTED_SUPABASE_PROJECT_REF]`).
- Regenerated `src/types/supabase.ts` — 11 enums + Constants block match the spec. Added `SUPABASE_URL` + `SUPABASE_ANON_KEY` stubs to `.env.example` and populated values into `.env` without echoing them.
- Files changed: `supabase/migrations/20260417080000_us_003_baseline_enums.sql`, `supabase/migrations/20260417080100_us_003_app_private_schema.sql`, `src/types/supabase.ts` (new; `.gitkeep` removed), `.env.example`, `.env` (local — gitignored)
- Quality checks: tsc ✓ / lint ✓ / supabase advisors ✓ (zero findings) / mobile-mcp n/a (DB-only story)
- Code-reviewer verdict: issues fixed in this commit (added `app_private` schema migration; clarified pg_cron comment)
- **Learnings for future iterations:**
  - Reviewer flagged missing `app_private` schema — §4.7's extension block includes schema + revoke. Future baseline migrations should follow §4.7 ordering even when the immediate story doesn't need the schema yet.
  - Security advisor WARNs on any extension in `public`; always pin to `extensions` schema except for `pg_cron`.
  - `apply_migration` stores the SQL body verbatim; editing the local `.sql` file afterwards drifts from the migrations table. If you must change DB state, add another migration.
---

## 2026-04-17 09:05 — US-004
- Applied `us_004_identity_tables` via Supabase MCP creating `public.users`, `public.creator_profiles` (all 9 denorm metric columns), `public.lister_profiles`, `public.social_links` (with the two partial unique indexes on `(user_id, platform)` and `(platform, handle)` scoped to `status <> 'unlinked'`).
- Regenerated `src/types/supabase.ts` — the four new tables appear under `Database["public"]["Tables"]`; enums unchanged from US-003.
- Files changed: `supabase/migrations/20260417090000_us_004_identity_tables.sql` (new), `src/types/supabase.ts`
- Quality checks: tsc ✓ / lint ✓ / supabase advisors ✓ (only INFO `rls_enabled_no_policy` — deferred to US-009 per story) / mobile-mcp n/a (DB-only story)
- Code-reviewer verdict: approved (no medium+ findings; field-for-field match against §4.7 lines 481–562)
- **Learnings for future iterations:**
  - Qualify `citext` as `extensions.citext` — default migration search_path does not include `extensions`.
  - Supabase auto-enables RLS on new `public.*` tables on this project; advisors show INFO `rls_enabled_no_policy` (not `rls_disabled`) until policies land in US-009. Harmless until then.
  - The spec's `create index on public.tbl (...)` (anonymous) is fine to keep verbatim — later migrations referring back to these indexes can use `pg_indexes` or rename at that point if needed.
---

## 2026-04-17 07:45 — US-002
- Installed 13 core runtime deps via `bunx expo install` (Supabase JS, AsyncStorage, url-polyfill, expo-router, Reanimated, gesture-handler, svg, lucide, haptics, linking, constants, font, mmkv); also added `react-native-worklets` explicitly since babel references `react-native-worklets/plugin`.
- Configured `babel.config.js` with `react-native-worklets/plugin`; flipped `package.json#main` to `expo-router/entry`; added minimal `app/_layout.tsx` (GestureHandlerRootView + Slot) and `app/index.tsx`; deleted orphaned `App.tsx` + `index.ts`.
- Files changed: `package.json`, `bun.lock`, `app.json` (auto-added expo-router/expo-font config plugins), `babel.config.js`, `app/_layout.tsx`, `app/index.tsx`, `App.tsx` (deleted), `index.ts` (deleted), `scripts/ralph/screenshots/us-002-boot.png`
- Quality checks: tsc ✓ / lint ✓ / supabase advisors n/a / mobile-mcp ✓ (booted iPhone 17 Pro sim, Expo Go loaded bundle, "Marketify" renders on cream background — see `scripts/ralph/screenshots/us-002-boot.png`)
- Code-reviewer verdict: issues fixed in this commit (added `react-native-worklets` explicit dep)
- Spec gap: story title mentions NativeWind but the explicit install list does not include it; specs (design.md, product-plan.md, tech-architecture.md) don't mandate NativeWind either. Skipping for now — design tokens (US-022) + StyleSheet is a viable path. Human to confirm.
- **Learnings for future iterations:**
  - Expo Go cannot load screens that require the `<Stack>` navigator from expo-router 6 under new-arch — use `<Slot />` or a dev-client build
  - `bunx expo install <pkg>` pins SDK-compatible versions; it just downgraded transitive worklets 0.8.1 → 0.5.1 to match SDK 54. Trust it.
  - `bunx expo install expo-router` auto-appends `"plugins": ["expo-router", ...]` to `app.json`
  - mobile-mcp's `mobile_open_url` refuses non-http(s) schemes; use `xcrun simctl openurl <udid> exp://...` to deep-link into Expo Go
---

## 2026-04-17 10:30 — US-005
- Applied `us_005_listing_tables` via Supabase MCP: `public.listings` (20 cols incl. plain-integer `min_followers_tiktok`/`_instagram` with partial btree indexes on `status='active'`), `public.listing_versions` with `(listing_id, version_number)` unique, self-FK `previous_version_id`, and the circular FK `listings.current_version_id → listing_versions.id` closed via a post-create `alter table`.
- Added `public.listing_conditions` (data-driven pre+post rows discriminated by `condition_kind` enum per spec §4.7 block 8, not separate `_pre_`/`_post_` tables as the story text suggested) and `public.sample_videos` (§4.7 block 9, same version-scoped grouping).
- Files changed: `supabase/migrations/20260417100000_us_005_listing_tables.sql` (new), `src/types/supabase.ts`
- Quality checks: tsc ✓ / lint ✓ / supabase advisors ✓ (only INFO `rls_enabled_no_policy` on the 4 new tables — expected; policies land in US-009) / mobile-mcp n/a (DB-only story)
- Code-reviewer verdict: approved (one cosmetic note on explicit vs anonymous index naming — no functional impact, not fixed)
- Spec gap: Story acceptance said `listing_pre_conditions` + `listing_post_conditions`; spec §4.7 block 8 defines a single `listing_conditions` table discriminated by the `condition_kind` enum. Followed the spec. Human to acknowledge if we actually wanted split tables — unlikely given the cascade trigger and eligibility engine both read from the single table.
- **Learnings for future iterations:**
  - When a story's table-naming contradicts the spec DDL, the spec wins (per Ralph instructions) — document as a Spec gap in the migration header + progress.txt.
  - `sample_videos` belongs with listing_versions (version-scoped FK); bundle it with the listing-tables migration rather than deferring — cascade trigger in US-010 treats "sample videos" as a versioned field.
  - Anonymous `create index on public.tbl (...)` in the spec is a style choice, not a requirement. Explicit names are fine and arguably better for later reindex operations; the reviewer flagged it as a benign deviation.
---

## 2026-04-17 11:15 — US-006
- Applied `us_006_pipeline_tables` via Supabase MCP: `public.applications` (10 cols, 3 indexes incl. partial-unique `applications_open_uniq` on `(listing_id, creator_id) where status in ('pending','approved')`), `public.submissions` (11 cols incl. reserved override audit trail + `submissions_override_requires_approved` check), `public.submission_videos`, and `public.submission_reuse_view` for the ReuseBadge aggregate.
- Follow-up migration `us_006_submission_reuse_view_invoker` flips the view to `security_invoker=on` to clear the advisor ERROR. Reviewer flagged that this interacts with US-009 RLS — promoted to Codebase Patterns as a US-009 handoff.
- Files changed: `supabase/migrations/20260417110000_us_006_pipeline_tables.sql` (new), `supabase/migrations/20260417110100_us_006_submission_reuse_view_invoker.sql` (new), `src/types/supabase.ts`
- Quality checks: tsc ✓ / lint ✓ / supabase advisors ✓ (zero ERROR-level after the security_invoker fix; only INFO `rls_enabled_no_policy` on the 3 new tables — expected, policies land in US-009) / mobile-mcp n/a (DB-only story)
- Code-reviewer verdict: approved with one US-009 handoff (see Codebase Patterns). Byte-for-byte match on DDL vs. spec §4.7 blocks 10-11.
- Spec gap: Story AC mentions "the reserved revision-request column on submissions" but spec explicitly states "No `revision_requested` in v1" (§2.2 line 121, §14.2 line 2014). The reserved columns per spec are `override_by_user_id` + `override_reason` (override audit trail, design §4.6 OverrideEligibilityDialog). Followed the spec; documented in the migration header.
- **Learnings for future iterations:**
  - Default Supabase views trip the `security_definer_view` advisor — always add `alter view <name> set (security_invoker = on)` in the same migration group.
  - When a spec contradicts itself (inline DDL comment vs. global standard), follow the global standard and flag the local contradiction as a future-story handoff rather than trying to reconcile in the current story.
  - The reviewer's "would break US-009" style of foresight is the value-add — consume it; don't fix downstream concerns in the current story (one-story-per-iteration) but do promote them so the next story inherits the context.
---

## 2026-04-17 12:05 — US-007
- Applied `us_007_metric_snapshots` via Supabase MCP: `public.metric_snapshots` (15 cols, base index `(social_link_id, fetched_at desc)`, three partial indexes — `is_latest` unique per (social_link_id, scrape_mode), `apify_run_id` unique excluding nulls, `status='refreshing'` for the janitor cron). Two trigger functions in `app_private`: `check_metric_snapshot_coherence` (BEFORE INSERT/UPDATE — rejects scrape_mode/platform mismatches) and `denorm_metrics` (BEFORE INSERT, SECURITY DEFINER — pg_advisory_xact_lock keyed on `(social_link_id, scrape_mode)`, demotes prior latest, mirrors owned columns into `creator_profiles`, refuses to roll `metrics_fetched_at` backward via `greatest()`).
- Three follow-up migrations were needed to satisfy `function_search_path_mutable` advisor + the GREATEST/now() qualification gotcha: `us_007_pin_function_search_path` (ALTER FUNCTION ... SET search_path = ''), `us_007_qualify_function_refs` (rewrites function bodies with `public.<type>` casts but introduced a real bug — `pg_catalog.greatest()` doesn't resolve), `us_007_fix_function_refs` (final correct bodies with bare `greatest()`/`now()` and restated `set search_path = ''` since CREATE OR REPLACE drops prior SET clauses). Per the append-only migrations rule, the broken middle file was retained verbatim with a header comment pointing to the fix migration — drift between local file and `supabase_migrations.schema_migrations` would be far worse than a documented intermediate state.
- End-to-end verified via `execute_sql`: 2 fresh tiktok_profile snapshots → newest claims `is_latest`, older demoted, denorm columns reflect newest values; coherence trigger rejects `ig_details` on a tiktok platform link; `refreshing` status row neither claims `is_latest` nor clobbers denorm; IG split keeps both `ig_details` AND `ig_posts` rows on the same link with `is_latest=true` (count=2) without overwriting each other; duplicate insert on same `apify_run_id` raises `unique_violation`.
- Files changed: `supabase/migrations/20260417120000_us_007_metric_snapshots.sql`, `supabase/migrations/20260417120100_us_007_pin_function_search_path.sql`, `supabase/migrations/20260417120200_us_007_qualify_function_refs.sql`, `supabase/migrations/20260417120300_us_007_fix_function_refs.sql`, `src/types/supabase.ts`
- Quality checks: tsc ✓ / lint ✓ / supabase advisors ✓ (zero ERROR/WARN; only INFO `rls_enabled_no_policy` on `metric_snapshots` — deferred to US-009) / mobile-mcp n/a (DB-only story)
- Code-reviewer verdict: approved with no blocking issues. Two "should fix" items both stem from spec-level gaps (`docs/tech-architecture.md` §15) — promoted to Codebase Patterns as US-008 follow-ups: (1) out-of-order webhook stale-write on denorm columns even though `metrics_fetched_at` is guarded; (2) single-arg `hashtextextended` advisory lock has 64-bit collision space vs. the safer two-arg `pg_advisory_xact_lock(hashtext, hashtext)`. Neither is in scope for US-007 (one-story-per-iteration + spec-verbatim rules).
- Spec gap: story acceptance says advisory lock keyed on `(user_id, scrape_mode)`; spec §15 keys it on `(social_link_id, scrape_mode)`. Functionally equivalent (social_links has partial unique on `(user_id, platform)` constraining the family) — followed spec verbatim and noted in migration header.
- **Learnings for future iterations:**
  - The `set search_path = ''` + `public.<type>` qualification pattern is the new default for every PL/pgSQL function in this repo — promoted to Codebase Patterns as a single consolidated rule (with the GREATEST gotcha called out explicitly). Save future iterations the cost of rediscovering this through advisor warnings + 42883 errors at trigger fire time.
  - When a SECURITY DEFINER function lands in a story, the search_path hardening is not optional follow-up work — bake it into the original migration to avoid the 4-migration sequence we ended up with here.
  - Test trigger functions end-to-end via `execute_sql` DO blocks that capture assertions into a temp table — `RAISE NOTICE` doesn't surface through Supabase MCP.
  - Reviewer's "false sense of out-of-order protection" framing on `greatest()` is a great pattern: a guard that protects one column but leaves siblings unguarded is worse than no guard, because it implies safety. Watch for this shape in future denormalization triggers.

RLS enabled on all three; policies deferred to US-009. `revoke all on public.events from anon, authenticated` enforces app-role append-only per spec §4.4 line 1086.
- Regenerated `src/types/supabase.ts`: three new tables appear with correct shapes (events.id → `number` from bigserial; notifications.kind → `notification_kind` enum; push_tokens.platform → `string` since the spec uses a CHECK not an enum).
- Files changed: `supabase/migrations/20260417130000_us_008_notifications_events.sql` (new), `src/types/supabase.ts`
- Quality checks: tsc ✓ / lint ✓ / supabase advisors ✓ (zero WARN/ERROR; only INFO `rls_enabled_no_policy` on the 3 new tables + the 12 pre-existing — all deferred to US-009) / mobile-mcp n/a (DB-only story)
- Code-reviewer verdict: approved. One medium observation flagged: append-only enforcement scope for `events` — migration matches the spec literally but service_role retains UPDATE/DELETE grants. Promoted to Codebase Patterns as a US-016+ handoff (edge functions writing to events must discipline themselves to INSERT-only; no DB trigger backstop exists).
- Spec gap: Story AC names only "notifications and events tables"; spec §4.7 block 12 bundles `push_tokens` alongside `notifications`. Followed spec (same precedent as US-005's `listing_conditions` vs story's split pre/post). Push notifications are scoped to v1.1 per product-plan §3.1 line 120 — the table is idle but the schema shape is locked in now.
- **Learnings for future iterations:**
  - Tables that the spec defines for future-version features (e.g. push_tokens for v1.1) should still be created when they appear in the same §4.7 block as an in-scope table. Partial block creation causes drift that later iterations would have to reconcile.
  - `revoke all on <table> from anon, authenticated` + RLS-enabled + zero policies = full deny for app roles. Useful pattern for anything in `public` that must be service-role-only (events-style audit logs, internal job queues).
  - When the reviewer flags a concern that the spec itself codifies, surface it as a forward-looking Codebase Patterns handoff rather than a migration-time fix — fixing it in the story would drift from the spec, which has a stronger hard-rule in this repo.

437	### 4.4 RLS Model
438	
439	Custom JWT issued by our `/auth/*` edge functions (signed with project JWT secret). Payload:
440	```json
441	{ "sub": "<user_id>", "role": "creator|lister", "session_id": "<uuid>" }
442	```
443	RLS policies read `auth.jwt() ->> 'sub'`. JWT is signed by us, not user-editable. Safe for authz.
444	
445	### 4.5 Indexes
446	
447	- Feed: partial index `(status, created_at DESC) WHERE status='active'`.
448	- Eligibility pre-filter: generated columns `listings.min_followers_tiktok`, `min_followers_ig` btree-indexed.
449	- Application uniqueness: partial unique `(listing_id, creator_id) WHERE status in ('pending','approved')`.
450	- Notifications: `(user_id, read_at NULLS FIRST, created_at DESC)`.
451	
452	### 4.6 Triggers / Functions
453	
454	All in `app_private` schema (security definer).
455	
456	| Function | Trigger |
457	|---|---|
458	| `bump_listing_version()` | BEFORE UPDATE on `listings` — if versioned field changed, INSERT new version, cascade-cancel pending apps, emit notifications. |
459	| `denorm_metrics()` | AFTER INSERT on `metric_snapshots` where `is_latest=true` — updates `creator_profiles`. |
460	| `submission_side_effects()` | AFTER UPDATE on `submissions` when status → `approved` — increments count. (Auto-close-on-max deferred to v1.1.) |
461	| `emit_event()` | Helper called by state-changing functions. |

### 4.4 RLS Model

Custom JWT issued by our `/auth/*` edge functions (signed with project JWT secret). Payload:
```json
{ "sub": "<user_id>", "role": "creator|lister", "session_id": "<uuid>" }
```
RLS policies read `auth.jwt() ->> 'sub'`. JWT is signed by us, not user-editable. Safe for authz.

### 4.5 Indexes

- Feed: partial index `(status, created_at DESC) WHERE status='active'`.
- Eligibility pre-filter: generated columns `listings.min_followers_tiktok`, `min_followers_ig` btree-indexed.
- Application uniqueness: partial unique `(listing_id, creator_id) WHERE status in ('pending','approved')`.
- Notifications: `(user_id, read_at NULLS FIRST, created_at DESC)`.

### 4.6 Triggers / Functions

All in `app_private` schema (security definer).

| Function | Trigger |
|---|---|
| `bump_listing_version()` | BEFORE UPDATE on `listings` — if versioned field changed, INSERT new version, cascade-cancel pending apps, emit notifications. |
| `denorm_metrics()` | AFTER INSERT on `metric_snapshots` where `is_latest=true` — updates `creator_profiles`. |
| `submission_side_effects()` | AFTER UPDATE on `submissions` when status → `approved` — increments count. (Auto-close-on-max deferred to v1.1.) |
| `emit_event()` | Helper called by state-changing functions. |

### 4.7 Full Migration SQL

```sql
-- =========================================================
-- 0. Extensions
-- =========================================================
create extension if not exists "pgcrypto";
create extension if not exists "pg_cron";
create extension if not exists "citext";

create schema if not exists app_private;
revoke all on schema app_private from anon, authenticated;

-- =========================================================
-- 1. Enums  (see §4.2)
-- =========================================================
-- ... (as above)

-- =========================================================
-- 2. users
-- =========================================================
create table public.users (
  id         uuid primary key default gen_random_uuid(),
  role       user_role not null,
  username   citext not null unique,
  email      citext unique,              -- nullable for creators
  created_at timestamptz not null default now(),
  updated_at timestamptz not null default now(),
  deleted_at timestamptz
);
create index on public.users (role) where deleted_at is null;

-- =========================================================
-- 3. creator_profiles (denormalized)
-- =========================================================
create table public.creator_profiles (
  user_id                     uuid primary key references public.users(id) on delete cascade,
  display_name                text,
  bio                         text,
  country                     text,
  tiktok_follower_count       integer,
  tiktok_avg_views_last_10    integer,
  tiktok_total_likes          bigint,
  tiktok_video_count          integer,
  tiktok_is_verified          boolean,
  instagram_follower_count    integer,
  instagram_avg_views_last_10 integer,
  instagram_media_count       integer,
  metrics_fetched_at          timestamptz,
  created_at                  timestamptz not null default now(),
  updated_at                  timestamptz not null default now()
);
create index on public.creator_profiles (tiktok_follower_count);
create index on public.creator_profiles (instagram_follower_count);

-- =========================================================
-- 4. lister_profiles
-- =========================================================
create table public.lister_profiles (
  user_id     uuid primary key references public.users(id) on delete cascade,
  org_name    text not null,
  website_url text,
  logo_path   text,
  description text,
  created_at  timestamptz not null default now(),
  updated_at  timestamptz not null default now()
);

-- =========================================================
-- 5. social_links   (no OAuth fields — Apify-based)
-- =========================================================
create table public.social_links (
  id                      uuid primary key default gen_random_uuid(),
  user_id                 uuid not null references public.users(id) on delete cascade,
  platform                platform not null,
  handle                  citext not null,
  status                  social_link_status not null default 'linked',
  last_scrape_run_id      text,                      -- Apify run id
  last_scrape_attempt_at  timestamptz,
  last_scrape_error       text,
  fail_count              smallint not null default 0,
  -- Soft self-confirmation: set when the creator taps "Yes, that's me" on the
  -- first-scrape confirmation card (design §5.6). Used to gate the card's
  -- visibility. Reset to null on (un)link cycles so the card re-appears for

840	create or replace function public.current_user_id() returns uuid
841	language sql stable as $$
842	  select nullif(auth.jwt() ->> 'sub', '')::uuid
843	$$;
844	create or replace function public.current_user_role() returns user_role
845	language sql stable as $$
846	  select (auth.jwt() ->> 'role')::user_role
847	$$;
848	
849	-- Design principles:
850	--   1. Every policy filters by `current_user_id()` — JWT-derived, never query param.
851	--   2. Service-role writes (edge functions, triggers) bypass RLS via `security definer`
852	--      functions; no `bypassrls` role is used for application code.
853	--   3. Listers see applicants' *public* metrics (via `creator_profiles.public_fields`)
854	--      only when the creator has an application into one of the lister's own listings.
855	--   4. Creators never see other creators' submissions, even for the same listing.
856	--   5. `events` is append-only and service-role only — no application reads.
857	
858	-- ---------------- users ----------------
859	create policy users_self_select on public.users for select
860	  using (id = public.current_user_id());
861	create policy users_self_update on public.users for update
862	  using (id = public.current_user_id())
863	  with check (id = public.current_user_id());
864	-- Listers see bare minimum (id, username, avatar_url) of any creator who has
865	-- applied to one of their listings. Enforced via a SECURITY DEFINER view
866	-- `public.applicant_public_profiles` rather than a SELECT policy on users —
867	-- keeps the policy simple and prevents accidental column leakage.
868	
869	-- ---------------- creator_profiles ----------------
870	create policy creator_profiles_self_rw on public.creator_profiles
871	  for all using (user_id = public.current_user_id())
872	  with check (user_id = public.current_user_id());
873	create policy creator_profiles_lister_read on public.creator_profiles
874	  for select using (
875	    exists (
876	      select 1 from public.applications a
877	      join public.listings l on l.id = a.listing_id
878	      where a.creator_id = creator_profiles.user_id
879	        and l.lister_id = public.current_user_id()
880	    )
881	  );
882	
883	-- ---------------- lister_profiles ----------------
884	create policy lister_profiles_self_rw on public.lister_profiles
885	  for all using (user_id = public.current_user_id())
886	  with check (user_id = public.current_user_id());
887	-- Public read of lister_profiles.public_fields via a view, not a policy.
888	
889	-- ---------------- social_links ----------------
890	create policy social_links_self_rw on public.social_links
891	  for all using (user_id = public.current_user_id())
892	  with check (user_id = public.current_user_id());
893	create policy social_links_lister_read on public.social_links
894	  for select using (
895	    exists (
896	      select 1 from public.applications a
897	      join public.listings l on l.id = a.listing_id
898	      where a.creator_id = social_links.user_id
899	        and l.lister_id = public.current_user_id()
900	    )
901	  );
902	
903	-- ---------------- metric_snapshots ----------------
904	-- Only is_latest + status='fresh' rows are readable; history is service-role only.
905	create policy metric_snapshots_self_read on public.metric_snapshots for select
906	  using (
907	    is_latest and status = 'fresh'
908	    and exists (
909	      select 1 from public.social_links sl
910	      where sl.id = metric_snapshots.social_link_id
911	        and sl.user_id = public.current_user_id()
912	    )
913	  );
914	create policy metric_snapshots_lister_read on public.metric_snapshots for select
915	  using (
916	    is_latest and status = 'fresh'
917	    and exists (
918	      select 1 from public.social_links sl
919	      join public.applications a on a.creator_id = sl.user_id
920	      join public.listings l on l.id = a.listing_id
921	      where sl.id = metric_snapshots.social_link_id
922	        and l.lister_id = public.current_user_id()
923	    )
924	  );
925	-- Writes: service-role only (webhook receiver, denorm trigger).
926	
927	-- ---------------- listings ----------------
928	create policy listings_public_read on public.listings for select
929	  using (status = 'active');
930	create policy listings_owner_all on public.listings for all
931	  using (lister_id = public.current_user_id())
932	  with check (lister_id = public.current_user_id());
933	
934	-- ---------------- listing_versions ----------------
935	create policy listing_versions_read_if_listing_readable on public.listing_versions
936	  for select using (
937	    exists (
938	      select 1 from public.listings l
939	      where l.id = listing_versions.listing_id
940	        and (l.status = 'active' or l.lister_id = public.current_user_id())
941	    )
942	  );
943	-- Writes: service-role only (via request_listing_version_bump).
944	
945	-- ---------------- listing_conditions ----------------
946	create policy listing_conditions_read_if_version_readable on public.listing_conditions
947	  for select using (
948	    exists (
949	      select 1 from public.listing_versions v
950	      join public.listings l on l.id = v.listing_id
951	      where v.id = listing_conditions.listing_version_id
952	        and (l.status = 'active' or l.lister_id = public.current_user_id())
953	    )
954	  );
955	-- Writes: edge function uses service role after validating lister ownership.
956	
957	-- ---------------- sample_videos ----------------
958	create policy sample_videos_read_if_version_readable on public.sample_videos
959	  for select using (
960	    exists (
961	      select 1 from public.listing_versions v
962	      join public.listings l on l.id = v.listing_id
963	      where v.id = sample_videos.listing_version_id
964	        and (l.status = 'active' or l.lister_id = public.current_user_id())
965	    )
966	  );
967	
968	-- ---------------- applications ----------------
969	create policy applications_creator_rw on public.applications
970	  for all using (creator_id = public.current_user_id())
971	  with check (creator_id = public.current_user_id());
972	create policy applications_lister_read on public.applications for select
973	  using (
974	    exists (
975	      select 1 from public.listings l
976	      where l.id = applications.listing_id
977	        and l.lister_id = public.current_user_id()
978	    )
979	  );
980	create policy applications_lister_decide on public.applications for update
981	  using (
982	    exists (
983	      select 1 from public.listings l
984	      where l.id = applications.listing_id
985	        and l.lister_id = public.current_user_id()
986	    )
987	  )
988	  with check (
989	    exists (
990	      select 1 from public.listings l
991	      where l.id = applications.listing_id
992	        and l.lister_id = public.current_user_id()
993	    )
994	  );
995	
996	-- ---------------- submissions ----------------
997	create policy submissions_creator_rw on public.submissions
998	  for all using (
999	    exists (
1000	      select 1 from public.applications a
1001	      where a.id = submissions.application_id
1002	        and a.creator_id = public.current_user_id()
1003	    )
1004	  )
1005	  with check (
1006	    exists (
1007	      select 1 from public.applications a
1008	      where a.id = submissions.application_id
1009	        and a.creator_id = public.current_user_id()
1010	    )
1011	  );
1012	create policy submissions_lister_read on public.submissions for select
1013	  using (
1014	    exists (
1015	      select 1 from public.applications a
1016	      join public.listings l on l.id = a.listing_id
1017	      where a.id = submissions.application_id
1018	        and l.lister_id = public.current_user_id()
1019	    )
1020	  );
1021	create policy submissions_lister_decide on public.submissions for update
1022	  using (
1023	    exists (
1024	      select 1 from public.applications a
1025	      join public.listings l on l.id = a.listing_id
1026	      where a.id = submissions.application_id
1027	        and l.lister_id = public.current_user_id()
1028	    )
1029	  )
1030	  with check (
1031	    exists (
1032	      select 1 from public.applications a
1033	      join public.listings l on l.id = a.listing_id
1034	      where a.id = submissions.application_id
1035	        and l.lister_id = public.current_user_id()
1036	    )
1037	  );
1038	
1039	-- ---------------- submission_videos ----------------
1040	-- Join through submissions → applications → listings. Symmetric with submissions.
1041	create policy submission_videos_read on public.submission_videos for select
1042	  using (
1043	    exists (
1044	      select 1 from public.submissions s
1045	      join public.applications a on a.id = s.application_id
1046	      where s.id = submission_videos.submission_id
1047	        and (
1048	          a.creator_id = public.current_user_id()
1049	          or exists (
1050	            select 1 from public.listings l
1051	            where l.id = a.listing_id and l.lister_id = public.current_user_id()
1052	          )
1053	        )
1054	    )
1055	  );
1056	create policy submission_videos_creator_write on public.submission_videos for all
1057	  using (
1058	    exists (
1059	      select 1 from public.submissions s
1060	      join public.applications a on a.id = s.application_id
1061	      where s.id = submission_videos.submission_id
1062	        and a.creator_id = public.current_user_id()
1063	    )
1064	  )
1065	  with check (
1066	    exists (
1067	      select 1 from public.submissions s
1068	      join public.applications a on a.id = s.application_id
1069	      where s.id = submission_videos.submission_id
1070	        and a.creator_id = public.current_user_id()
1071	    )
1072	  );
1073	
1074	-- ---------------- notifications + push_tokens ----------------
1075	create policy notifications_self_rw on public.notifications for all
1076	  using (user_id = public.current_user_id())
1077	  with check (user_id = public.current_user_id());
1078	create policy push_tokens_self_rw on public.push_tokens for all
1079	  using (user_id = public.current_user_id())
1080	  with check (user_id = public.current_user_id());
1081	
1082	-- ---------------- events ----------------
1083	-- Service role only. No application read/write policy — the absence of any
1084	-- policy + RLS enabled = deny for all non-superuser roles.
1085	-- (Supabase's anon/authenticated roles have no grants to this table either.)
1086	revoke all on public.events from anon, authenticated;
1087

743	create table public.submission_videos (
744	  id                uuid primary key default gen_random_uuid(),
745	  submission_id     uuid not null references public.submissions(id) on delete cascade,
746	  platform          platform not null,
747	  url               text not null,

753	
754	-- "Also submitted to N other campaigns" chip (the ReuseBadge in design §4.6).
755	-- Aggregates how many other
756	-- submissions by the same creator use the same video URL (normalized by
757	-- external_id, not raw URL, so `?t=30s` variants collapse). The chip is only
758	-- shown to listers with RLS on submissions so they see their own submission_id
759	-- + a count — they never see WHICH other campaigns, only HOW MANY.
760	-- Security-def view so RLS on base tables doesn't mask the count:
761	-- the view returns aggregates, and we bake in the current lister filter.
762	create or replace view public.submission_reuse_view as
763	  select
764	    s.id as submission_id,
765	    count(other.id) filter (where other.id <> s.id) as reuse_count
766	  from public.submissions s
767	  join public.submission_videos sv
768	    on sv.submission_id = s.id
769	  left join public.submission_videos other_sv
770	    on other_sv.external_id = sv.external_id
771	   and other_sv.platform = sv.platform
772	   and other_sv.external_id is not null
773	  left join public.submissions other
774	    on other.id = other_sv.submission_id
775	   and other.id <> s.id
776	  group by s.id;
777	
778	comment on view public.submission_reuse_view is
779	  'Exposes per-submission count of other submissions reusing the same video external_id. '

where status in ('pending','approved');
create index on public.submissions (status, created_at desc);

create table public.submission_videos (
  id                uuid primary key default gen_random_uuid(),
  submission_id     uuid not null references public.submissions(id) on delete cascade,
  platform          platform not null,
  url               text not null,
  external_id       text,
  oembed_cached     jsonb,
  last_validated_at timestamptz,
  sort_order        smallint not null default 0
);

-- "Also submitted to N other campaigns" chip (the ReuseBadge in design §4.6).
-- Aggregates how many other
-- submissions by the same creator use the same video URL (normalized by
-- external_id, not raw URL, so `?t=30s` variants collapse). The chip is only
-- shown to listers with RLS on submissions so they see their own submission_id
-- + a count — they never see WHICH other campaigns, only HOW MANY.
-- Security-def view so RLS on base tables doesn't mask the count:
-- the view returns aggregates, and we bake in the current lister filter.
create or replace view public.submission_reuse_view as
  select
    s.id as submission_id,
    count(other.id) filter (where other.id <> s.id) as reuse_count
  from public.submissions s
  join public.submission_videos sv
    on sv.submission_id = s.id
  left join public.submission_videos other_sv
    on other_sv.external_id = sv.external_id
   and other_sv.platform = sv.platform
   and other_sv.external_id is not null
  left join public.submissions other
    on other.id = other_sv.submission_id
   and other.id <> s.id
  group by s.id;

comment on view public.submission_reuse_view is
  'Exposes per-submission count of other submissions reusing the same video external_id. '
  'Read via RLS on submissions — a lister sees reuse_count only for their own listings'' submissions.';
create index on public.submission_videos (submission_id);

-- =========================================================
-- 12. notifications + push_tokens
-- =========================================================
create table public.notifications (
  id         uuid primary key default gen_random_uuid(),
  user_id    uuid not null references public.users(id) on delete cascade,
  kind       notification_kind not null,
  payload    jsonb not null,
  read_at    timestamptz,
  created_at timestamptz not null default now()
);
create index notifications_inbox_idx on public.notifications (user_id, created_at desc);
create index notifications_unread_idx on public.notifications (user_id) where read_at is null;

create table public.push_tokens (
  id           uuid primary key default gen_random_uuid(),
  user_id      uuid not null references public.users(id) on delete cascade,
  expo_token   text not null unique,
  platform     text not null check (platform in ('ios','android')),
  created_at   timestamptz not null default now(),
  last_seen_at timestamptz not null default now()
);

-- =========================================================
-- 13. events (append-only audit log)
-- =========================================================
create table public.events (
  id         bigserial primary key,
  actor_id   uuid,
  entity     text not null,
  entity_id  uuid not null,
  action     text not null,
  old_state  jsonb,
  new_state  jsonb,
  created_at timestamptz not null default now()
);
create index on public.events (entity, entity_id, created_at desc);

-- =========================================================
-- 14. RLS (see §4.4)
-- =========================================================
alter table public.users enable row level security;

1086	revoke all on public.events from anon, authenticated;
1087	
1088	-- ---------------- Cross-tenant integration test plan ----------------
1089	-- (enforced by `tests/rls/*.sql` in CI, running as a JWT-minted session):
1090	-- 1. Creator A cannot SELECT Creator B's social_links, metric_snapshots,
1091	--    applications, submissions, notifications, push_tokens.
1092	-- 2. Lister X cannot SELECT Lister Y's listings (draft), applications,
1093	--    submissions, notifications — even for shared creators.
1094	-- 3. Creator A cannot SELECT Lister X's non-active listings.
1095	-- 4. A creator cannot INSERT a submission for an application they don't own.
1096	-- 5. A lister cannot UPDATE an application status for a listing they don't own.
1097	-- 6. Attempting to subscribe to a Realtime channel the user can't SELECT
1098	--    returns no rows (Supabase Realtime respects SELECT policies).
1099	
1100	-- =========================================================
1101	-- 15. Triggers
1102	-- =========================================================
1103	-- Core bump routine — called directly (from listings UPDATE trigger) or via the
1104	-- edge function PATCH /listings/:id after it has applied listing_conditions/sample_videos edits.
1105	-- The edge function is the sole writer to listing_conditions + sample_videos once a listing is active;
1106	-- it calls app_private.request_listing_version_bump(listing_id, reason, changed_fields) AFTER
1107	-- those child edits commit, which performs an UPDATE listings SET ... that fires this trigger.
1108	create or replace function app_private.bump_listing_version()
1109	returns trigger language plpgsql security definer as $$
1110	declare
1111	  versioned_changed boolean;
1112	  new_version_id    uuid;
1113	  prev_version_id   uuid;
1114	  changed_fields    text[] := array[]::text[];
1115	begin
1116	  if old.price_cents     is distinct from new.price_cents     then changed_fields := changed_fields || 'price_cents'; end if;
1117	  if old.currency        is distinct from new.currency        then changed_fields := changed_fields || 'currency'; end if;
1118	  if old.max_submissions is distinct from new.max_submissions then changed_fields := changed_fields || 'max_submissions'; end if;
1119	  -- The edge function passes a cue via the dummy `version_bump_reason` text column (set-and-reset in same UPDATE)
1120	  -- to indicate listing_conditions or sample_videos were edited:
1121	  if new.version_bump_reason is not null then
1122	    changed_fields := changed_fields || new.version_bump_reason;  -- e.g. 'conditions' | 'sample_videos'
1123	    new.version_bump_reason := null;  -- consume the cue
1124	  end if;
1125	
1126	  versioned_changed := array_length(changed_fields, 1) > 0;
1127	  if versioned_changed and new.status = 'active' then
1128	    prev_version_id      := old.current_version_id;
1129	    new.version_number   := old.version_number + 1;
1130	    insert into public.listing_versions(listing_id, version_number, price_cents, currency, max_submissions, snapshot, previous_version_id, changed_fields)

content.content:
1	-- =========================================================
2	-- US-009: RLS policies on every user-data table
3	-- Source of truth: docs/tech-architecture.md §4.4 + §4.7 block 14.
4	--
5	-- Behavior:
6	--   * Per-tenant scoping via public.current_user_id() → auth.jwt() ->> 'sub'.
7	--   * RLS is (re)enabled on all 15 tables enumerated in §4.7 block 14.
8	--   * public.events has no policy (RLS + zero policies + revoke = deny);
9	--     service-role writes bypass via security-definer functions.
10	--   * Story AC named `listing_pre_conditions` + `listing_post_conditions`, but
11	--     the spec uses a single `listing_conditions` table discriminated by kind
12	--     (§4.7 block 8). Policies attach to `listing_conditions` per spec.
13	--
14	-- submission_reuse_view handoff (Codebase Patterns):
15	--   The view at public.submission_reuse_view (us_006) runs with
16	--   security_invoker = on to clear the `security_definer_view` advisor, but
17	--   under that mode a lister's RLS on submissions would mask cross-lister
18	--   reuse counts — defeating the ReuseBadge. We drop the view and replace it
19	--   with public.submission_reuse_count(uuid), a SECURITY DEFINER function that
20	--   performs its own caller-authorization check (must own the listing behind
21	--   the submission) and then counts across all submissions, bypassing RLS.
22	--   The UI stories (US-058, US-065) call this via PostgREST RPC.
23	-- =========================================================
24	
25	-- ---------------- Helpers (JWT → current user / role) ----------------
26	create or replace function public.current_user_id()
27	returns uuid
28	language sql
29	stable
30	set search_path = ''
31	as $$
32	  select nullif(auth.jwt() ->> 'sub', '')::uuid
33	$$;
34	
35	create or replace function public.current_user_role()
36	returns public.user_role
37	language sql
38	stable
39	set search_path = ''
40	as $$
41	  select nullif(auth.jwt() ->> 'role', '')::public.user_role
42	$$;
43	
44	revoke all on function public.current_user_id() from public;
45	revoke all on function public.current_user_role() from public;
46	grant execute on function public.current_user_id() to anon, authenticated, service_role;
47	grant execute on function public.current_user_role() to anon, authenticated, service_role;
48	
49	-- ---------------- Enable RLS (idempotent; Supabase already auto-enabled) ----------------
50	alter table public.users              enable row level security;
51	alter table public.creator_profiles   enable row level security;
52	alter table public.lister_profiles    enable row level security;
53	alter table public.social_links       enable row level security;
54	alter table public.metric_snapshots   enable row level security;
55	alter table public.listings           enable row level security;
56	alter table public.listing_versions   enable row level security;
57	alter table public.listing_conditions enable row level security;
58	alter table public.sample_videos      enable row level security;
59	alter table public.applications       enable row level security;
60	alter table public.submissions        enable row level security;
61	alter table public.submission_videos  enable row level security;
62	alter table public.notifications      enable row level security;
63	alter table public.push_tokens        enable row level security;
64	alter table public.events             enable row level security;
65	
66	-- ---------------- users ----------------
67	drop policy if exists users_self_select on public.users;
68	create policy users_self_select on public.users for select
69	  using (id = public.current_user_id());
70	
71	drop policy if exists users_self_update on public.users;
72	create policy users_self_update on public.users for update
73	  using (id = public.current_user_id())
74	  with check (id = public.current_user_id());
75	
76	-- ---------------- creator_profiles ----------------
77	drop policy if exists creator_profiles_self_rw on public.creator_profiles;
78	create policy creator_profiles_self_rw on public.creator_profiles
79	  for all
80	  using (user_id = public.current_user_id())
81	  with check (user_id = public.current_user_id());
82	
83	drop policy if exists creator_profiles_lister_read on public.creator_profiles;
84	create policy creator_profiles_lister_read on public.creator_profiles
85	  for select using (
86	    exists (
87	      select 1
88	      from public.applications a
89	      join public.listings l on l.id = a.listing_id
90	      where a.creator_id = public.creator_profiles.user_id
91	        and l.lister_id = public.current_user_id()
92	    )
93	  );
94	
95	-- ---------------- lister_profiles ----------------
96	drop policy if exists lister_profiles_self_rw on public.lister_profiles;
97	create policy lister_profiles_self_rw on public.lister_profiles
98	  for all
99	  using (user_id = public.current_user_id())
100	  with check (user_id = public.current_user_id());
101	
102	-- ---------------- social_links ----------------
103	drop policy if exists social_links_self_rw on public.social_links;
104	create policy social_links_self_rw on public.social_links
105	  for all
106	  using (user_id = public.current_user_id())
107	  with check (user_id = public.current_user_id());
108	
109	drop policy if exists social_links_lister_read on public.social_links;
110	create policy social_links_lister_read on public.social_links
111	  for select using (
112	    exists (
113	      select 1
114	      from public.applications a
115	      join public.listings l on l.id = a.listing_id
116	      where a.creator_id = public.social_links.user_id
117	        and l.lister_id = public.current_user_id()
118	    )
119	  );
120	
121	-- ---------------- metric_snapshots ----------------
122	-- Only is_latest + status='fresh' rows are readable; history is service-role only.
123	drop policy if exists metric_snapshots_self_read on public.metric_snapshots;
124	create policy metric_snapshots_self_read on public.metric_snapshots for select
125	  using (
126	    is_latest and status = 'fresh'
127	    and exists (
128	      select 1 from public.social_links sl
129	      where sl.id = public.metric_snapshots.social_link_id
130	        and sl.user_id = public.current_user_id()
131	    )
132	  );
133	
134	drop policy if exists metric_snapshots_lister_read on public.metric_snapshots;
135	create policy metric_snapshots_lister_read on public.metric_snapshots for select
136	  using (
137	    is_latest and status = 'fresh'
138	    and exists (
139	      select 1
140	      from public.social_links sl
141	      join public.applications a on a.creator_id = sl.user_id
142	      join public.listings l on l.id = a.listing_id
143	      where sl.id = public.metric_snapshots.social_link_id
144	        and l.lister_id = public.current_user_id()
145	    )
146	  );
147	
148	-- ---------------- listings ----------------
149	drop policy if exists listings_public_read on public.listings;
150	create policy listings_public_read on public.listings for select
151	  using (status = 'active');
152	
153	drop policy if exists listings_owner_all on public.listings;
154	create policy listings_owner_all on public.listings
155	  for all
156	  using (lister_id = public.current_user_id())
157	  with check (lister_id = public.current_user_id());
158	
159	-- ---------------- listing_versions ----------------
160	drop policy if exists listing_versions_read_if_listing_readable on public.listing_versions;
161	create policy listing_versions_read_if_listing_readable on public.listing_versions
162	  for select using (
163	    exists (
164	      select 1 from public.listings l
165	      where l.id = public.listing_versions.listing_id
166	        and (l.status = 'active' or l.lister_id = public.current_user_id())
167	    )
168	  );
169	
170	-- ---------------- listing_conditions ----------------
171	drop policy if exists listing_conditions_read_if_version_readable on public.listing_conditions;
172	create policy listing_conditions_read_if_version_readable on public.listing_conditions
173	  for select using (
174	    exists (
175	      select 1
176	      from public.listing_versions v
177	      join public.listings l on l.id = v.listing_id
178	      where v.id = public.listing_conditions.listing_version_id
179	        and (l.status = 'active' or l.lister_id = public.current_user_id())
180	    )
181	  );
182	
183	-- ---------------- sample_videos ----------------
184	drop policy if exists sample_videos_read_if_version_readable on public.sample_videos;
185	create policy sample_videos_read_if_version_readable on public.sample_videos
186	  for select using (
187	    exists (
188	      select 1
189	      from public.listing_versions v
190	      join public.listings l on l.id = v.listing_id
191	      where v.id = public.sample_videos.listing_version_id
192	        and (l.status = 'active' or l.lister_id = public.current_user_id())
193	    )
194	  );
195	
196	-- ---------------- applications ----------------
197	drop policy if exists applications_creator_rw on public.applications;
198	create policy applications_creator_rw on public.applications
199	  for all
200	  using (creator_id = public.current_user_id())
201	  with check (creator_id = public.current_user_id());
202	
203	drop policy if exists applications_lister_read on public.applications;
204	create policy applications_lister_read on public.applications for select
205	  using (
206	    exists (
207	      select 1 from public.listings l
208	      where l.id = public.applications.listing_id
209	        and l.lister_id = public.current_user_id()
210	    )
211	  );
212	
213	drop policy if exists applications_lister_decide on public.applications;
214	create policy applications_lister_decide on public.applications for update
215	  using (
216	    exists (
217	      select 1 from public.listings l
218	      where l.id = public.applications.listing_id
219	        and l.lister_id = public.current_user_id()
220	    )
221	  )
222	  with check (
223	    exists (
224	      select 1 from public.listings l
225	      where l.id = public.applications.listing_id
226	        and l.lister_id = public.current_user_id()
227	    )
228	  );
229	
230	-- ---------------- submissions ----------------
231	drop policy if exists submissions_creator_rw on public.submissions;
232	create policy submissions_creator_rw on public.submissions
233	  for all
234	  using (
235	    exists (
236	      select 1 from public.applications a
237	      where a.id = public.submissions.application_id
238	        and a.creator_id = public.current_user_id()
239	    )
240	  )
241	  with check (
242	    exists (
243	      select 1 from public.applications a
244	      where a.id = public.submissions.application_id
245	        and a.creator_id = public.current_user_id()
246	    )
247	  );
248	
249	drop policy if exists submissions_lister_read on public.submissions;
250	create policy submissions_lister_read on public.submissions for select
251	  using (
252	    exists (
253	      select 1
254	      from public.applications a
255	      join public.listings l on l.id = a.listing_id
256	      where a.id = public.submissions.application_id
257	        and l.lister_id = public.current_user_id()
258	    )
259	  );
260	
261	drop policy if exists submissions_lister_decide on public.submissions;
262	create policy submissions_lister_decide on public.submissions for update
263	  using (
264	    exists (
265	      select 1
266	      from public.applications a
267	      join public.listings l on l.id = a.listing_id
268	      where a.id = public.submissions.application_id
269	        and l.lister_id = public.current_user_id()
270	    )
271	  )
272	  with check (
273	    exists (
274	      select 1
275	      from public.applications a
276	      join public.listings l on l.id = a.listing_id
277	      where a.id = public.submissions.application_id
278	        and l.lister_id = public.current_user_id()
279	    )
280	  );
281	
282	-- ---------------- submission_videos ----------------
283	drop policy if exists submission_videos_read on public.submission_videos;
284	create policy submission_videos_read on public.submission_videos for select
285	  using (
286	    exists (
287	      select 1
288	      from public.submissions s
289	      join public.applications a on a.id = s.application_id
290	      where s.id = public.submission_videos.submission_id
291	        and (
292	          a.creator_id = public.current_user_id()
293	          or exists (
294	            select 1 from public.listings l
295	            where l.id = a.listing_id
296	              and l.lister_id = public.current_user_id()
297	          )
298	        )
299	    )
300	  );
301	
302	drop policy if exists submission_videos_creator_write on public.submission_videos;
303	create policy submission_videos_creator_write on public.submission_videos
304	  for all
305	  using (
306	    exists (
307	      select 1
308	      from public.submissions s
309	      join public.applications a on a.id = s.application_id
310	      where s.id = public.submission_videos.submission_id
311	        and a.creator_id = public.current_user_id()
312	    )
313	  )
314	  with check (
315	    exists (
316	      select 1
317	      from public.submissions s
318	      join public.applications a on a.id = s.application_id
319	      where s.id = public.submission_videos.submission_id
320	        and a.creator_id = public.current_user_id()
321	    )
322	  );
323	
324	-- ---------------- notifications + push_tokens ----------------
325	drop policy if exists notifications_self_rw on public.notifications;
326	create policy notifications_self_rw on public.notifications
327	  for all
328	  using (user_id = public.current_user_id())
329	  with check (user_id = public.current_user_id());
330	
331	drop policy if exists push_tokens_self_rw on public.push_tokens;
332	create policy push_tokens_self_rw on public.push_tokens
333	  for all
334	  using (user_id = public.current_user_id())
335	  with check (user_id = public.current_user_id());
336	
337	-- ---------------- events ----------------
338	-- Service role only. No application read/write policy — the absence of any
339	-- policy + RLS enabled = deny for all non-superuser roles (spec §4.7 block 14).
340	revoke all on public.events from anon, authenticated;
341	
342	-- =========================================================
343	-- submission_reuse: view → function swap (see header)
344	-- =========================================================
345	drop view if exists public.submission_reuse_view;
346	
347	create or replace function public.submission_reuse_count(p_submission_id uuid)
348	returns integer
349	language plpgsql
350	security definer
351	stable
352	set search_path = ''
353	as $$
354	declare
355	  v_caller   uuid;
356	  v_allowed  boolean;
357	  v_count    integer;
358	begin
359	  v_caller := public.current_user_id();
360	  if v_caller is null then
361	    return null;
362	  end if;
363	
364	  -- Only the lister whose listing hosts this submission can read the count.
365	  select exists (
366	    select 1
367	    from public.submissions s
368	    join public.applications a on a.id = s.application_id
369	    join public.listings l on l.id = a.listing_id
370	    where s.id = p_submission_id
371	      and l.lister_id = v_caller
372	  ) into v_allowed;
373	
374	  if not v_allowed then
375	    return null;
376	  end if;
377	
378	  select count(other.id)::integer
379	  into v_count
380	  from public.submission_videos sv
381	  join public.submission_videos other_sv
382	    on other_sv.external_id = sv.external_id
383	   and other_sv.platform    = sv.platform
384	   and other_sv.external_id is not null
385	  join public.submissions other
386	    on other.id = other_sv.submission_id
387	   and other.id <> sv.submission_id
388	  where sv.submission_id = p_submission_id;
389	
390	  return coalesce(v_count, 0);
391	end;
392	$$;
393	
394	comment on function public.submission_reuse_count(uuid) is
395	  'ReuseBadge source (design §4.6). Returns the number of *other* submissions '
396	  'sharing this submission''s video (external_id + platform), across all listers. '
397	  'Security-definer: own auth check — only the owning lister sees a value; '
398	  'non-owners get NULL.';
399	
400	revoke all on function public.submission_reuse_count(uuid) from public;
401	grant execute on function public.submission_reuse_count(uuid) to authenticated, service_role;

# Marketify — Ralph Agent Instructions  You are an autonomous coding agent building **Marketify**, a mobile marketplace where creators claim bounties from listers (Expo + React Native + Supabase). Eac…

message.content:
This session is being continued from a previous conversation that ran out of context. The summary below covers the earlier portion of the conversation.

Summary:
1. Primary Request and Intent:
   The user invoked the Ralph autonomous loop for Marketify (mobile Expo + RN + Supabase marketplace). The task is to execute ONE iteration of the loop per `scripts/ralph/CLAUDE.md`: pick the lowest-priority user story with `passes: false` from `scripts/ralph/prd.json`, implement it against the spec in `docs/`, run quality gates + code review + (if UI) mobile verification, commit, and mark it done. The story for this iteration is US-009 — "Migration: RLS policies on every user-data table" — which enables RLS and writes policies per `docs/tech-architecture.md` §4.4 + §4.7 block 14, verifies zero error-level security advisors, and proves cross-user reads are blocked via a fake-JWT test.

2. Key Technical Concepts:
   - Supabase Postgres RLS (row-level security) policies with `auth.jwt()` ->> claims
   - Custom JWT approach (spec says signed with "project JWT secret"; US-013 says `MARKETIFY_JWT_SECRET` — known latent conflict, deferred)
   - Trigger/function hardening: `set search_path = ''` + schema-qualify all user-defined refs (pattern from prior iterations)
   - `security_invoker=on` on views vs SECURITY DEFINER functions — advisor `security_definer_view` flags the latter, hence the view → function swap
   - Supabase MCP tools (`apply_migration`, `execute_sql`, `get_advisors`, `generate_typescript_types`, `list_migrations`)
   - PL/pgSQL `set local role authenticated` + `set local "request.jwt.claims"` pattern for RLS testing in a transaction with ROLLBACK
   - Append-only migration discipline (migrations named `us_NNN_description`)
   - Supabase project: `marketify`, ref `[REDACTED_SUPABASE_PROJECT_REF]`, region `ap-southeast-1`

3. Files and Code Sections:
   - `/Users/bulutyazgan/Desktop/marketify/scripts/ralph/prd.json`
     - Read to identify US-009 as next story (lowest priority with `passes: false`).
   - `/Users/bulutyazgan/Desktop/marketify/scripts/ralph/progress.txt`
     - Read for Codebase Patterns, especially the US-009 TODO about `submission_reuse_view` correctness under RLS.
   - `/Users/bulutyazgan/Desktop/marketify/docs/tech-architecture.md`
     - Read §4.4 (RLS model: JWT-derived `auth.jwt() ->> 'sub'`) and §4.7 block 14 (RLS DDL for 15 tables + all policies).
   - `/Users/bulutyazgan/Desktop/marketify/supabase/migrations/20260417140000_us_009_rls_policies.sql` (NEW, 401 lines)
     - Created helpers: `public.current_user_id()` and `public.current_user_role()` (SQL, stable, `set search_path = ''`, qualified enum casts). Example:
       ```sql
       create or replace function public.current_user_id()
       returns uuid language sql stable set search_path = '' as $
         select nullif(auth.jwt() ->> 'sub', '')::uuid
       $;
       ```
     - `alter table ... enable row level security` on all 15 tables (idempotent).
     - Full policy set per spec: `users_self_select/update`, `creator_profiles_self_rw` + `creator_profiles_lister_read`, `lister_profiles_self_rw`, `social_links_self_rw` + `_lister_read`, `metric_snapshots_self_read` + `_lister_read`, `listings_public_read` + `listings_owner_all`, `listing_versions_read_if_listing_readable`, `listing_conditions_read_if_version_readable`, `sample_videos_read_if_version_readable`, `applications_creator_rw` + `_lister_read` + `_lister_decide`, `submissions_creator_rw` + `_lister_read` + `_lister_decide`, `submission_videos_read` + `_creator_write`, `notifications_self_rw`, `push_tokens_self_rw`. Events: no policy + `revoke all ... from anon, authenticated`.
     - View → function swap: dropped `public.submission_reuse_view`; created SECURITY DEFINER `public.submission_reuse_count(p_submission_id uuid) returns integer` with its own caller-auth check (returns NULL to non-owning listers):
       ```sql
       create or replace function public.submission_reuse_count(p_submission_id uuid)
       returns integer language plpgsql security definer stable set search_path = '' as $
       declare v_caller uuid; v_allowed boolean; v_count integer;
       begin
         v_caller := public.current_user_id();
         if v_caller is null then return null; end if;
         select exists (select 1 from public.submissions s
                        join public.applications a on a.id = s.application_id
                        join public.listings l on l.id = a.listing_id
                        where s.id = p_submission_id and l.lister_id = v_caller)
           into v_allowed;
         if not v_allowed then return null; end if;
         select count(other.id)::integer into v_count
         from public.submission_videos sv
         join public.submission_videos other_sv
           on other_sv.external_id = sv.external_id
          and other_sv.platform = sv.platform
          and other_sv.external_id is not null
         join public.submissions other
           on other.id = other_sv.submission_id
          and other.id <> sv.submission_id
         where sv.submission_id = p_submission_id;
         return coalesce(v_count, 0);
       end; $;
       revoke all on function public.submission_reuse_count(uuid) from public;
       grant execute on function public.submission_reuse_count(uuid) to authenticated, service_role;
       ```
   - `/Users/bulutyazgan/Desktop/marketify/src/types/supabase.ts` (REGENERATED + written)
     - Now includes `Functions: { current_user_id, current_user_role, submission_reuse_count }`. Views section empty (reuse_view dropped).

4. Errors and fixes:
   - Test SQL: `perform 1 from public.events limit 1;` outside a DO block — Postgres syntax error (PERFORM is PL/pgSQL-only). **Fix**: removed that events exception test; verified events access separately via `information_schema.role_table_grants` confirming only service_role has grants.
   - Test SQL: `permission denied for table _asserts` when `authenticated` role tried to INSERT into the temp table. **Fix**: added `grant insert, select on _asserts to authenticated;` before switching to authenticated role.
   - Test SQL: `ERROR: 42703: column "listing_version_id" of relation "submissions" does not exist`. Confirmed via `information_schema.columns` that `submissions` has columns `id, application_id, status, cover_note, created_at, updated_at, decided_at, decision_note, override_by_user_id, override_reason` — no `listing_version_id` (that column is on `applications`, not `submissions`). **Fix**: removed the column from the submissions INSERT in the test.
   - Write tool rejected initial `src/types/supabase.ts` write with "File has not been read yet." **Fix**: called Read on the file first, then Write succeeded.

5. Problem Solving:
   - Resolved the `submission_reuse_view` handoff (documented in Codebase Patterns from US-006) by replacing the view with a SECURITY DEFINER function that bypasses RLS to count across all listers while gating access via its own ownership check. This avoids both the `security_definer_view` advisor error (view would trigger it) and the RLS-masking problem (security_invoker=on would hide cross-lister reuses).
   - Verified RLS via a single self-contained `begin; ... rollback;` test that seeds 4 users (2 creators, 2 listers), 2 listings (1 active, 1 draft), 1 application, then impersonates each user via `set local role authenticated` + `set local "request.jwt.claims"` and asserts expected row visibility. All 11 RLS assertions passed. A second test of 4 submission_reuse_count assertions also passed.
   - Security advisor post-migration: only INFO `rls_enabled_no_policy` on `public.events` remains (intentional: spec §4.7 block 14 says events is service-role only, no policy + revoke = deny).

6. All user messages:
   - Initial Ralph loop invocation (implicit — no user typed message; the conversation started with system reminders bundling `scripts/ralph/CLAUDE.md`, CLAUDE.md, memory, and environment context).
   - Three interstitial system reminders reminding to consider using TodoWrite (non-substantive; explicitly marked as gentle reminders to ignore if not applicable).
   - CRITICAL final user message: "Respond with TEXT ONLY. Do NOT call any tools. ... create a detailed summary of the conversation so far." (this message)

7. Pending Tasks:
   - Run quality gates: `npx tsc --noEmit` (must pass zero errors), `npx expo lint` (must pass).
   - Spawn `feature-dev:code-reviewer` subagent against the diff scope (migration + regenerated types), story acceptance criteria, and doc sections §4.4 + §4.7 block 14. Address every medium+ issue and re-run quality gates after fixes.
   - Commit the migration + regenerated types with message `feat: [US-009] Migration: RLS policies on every user-data table` (list paths explicitly; no `git add -A`).
   - In `prd.json`, set US-009 `passes: true`.
   - Append a dated entry to `scripts/ralph/progress.txt` with the US-009 section following the required format (what was implemented, files changed, quality checks, code-reviewer verdict, learnings). Promote any general-reusable learnings into the `## Codebase Patterns` section at the top.
